tasks.description: Provision all infrastructure components required by AlertHub services including databases, caches, message queues, and storage using Kubernetes operators
tasks.details:
1. Deploy PostgreSQL cluster using CloudNative-PG operator:
   - Create Cluster CR with 1 instance, 20Gi storage
   - Initialize 'alerthub' database with 'alerthub_user' owner
   - Configure connection pooling (max_connections: 200)
   - Set resource limits (512Mi-2Gi memory, 250m-1000m CPU)

2. Deploy Redis/Valkey using Redis Operator:
   - Create Redis CR with valkey:7.2-alpine image
   - Configure 5Gi persistent storage with mayastor
   - Set resource limits (256Mi-1Gi memory, 100m-500m CPU)

3. Deploy Kafka using Strimzi operator:
   - Create Kafka CR with version 3.8.0, 1 replica
   - Configure internal listener on port 9092
   - Create topics: alerthub.notifications.created (6 partitions), alerthub.notifications.delivered (3 partitions), alerthub.notifications.failed (3 partitions)
   - Set retention to 7 days for created/delivered, 14 days for failed
   - Configure 20Gi persistent storage

4. Deploy MongoDB using Percona operator:
   - Create PerconaServerMongoDB CR with version 7.0.14-8
   - Configure replica set 'rs0' with 1 member
   - Set 10Gi persistent storage with mayastor

5. Deploy RabbitMQ using RabbitMQ operator:
   - Create RabbitmqCluster CR with 1 replica
   - Configure 10Gi persistent storage
   - Set resource limits (512Mi-1Gi memory, 200m-500m CPU)

6. Setup SeaweedFS for S3-compatible storage:
   - Deploy SeaweedFS filer service
   - Create buckets: alerthub-attachments, alerthub-exports, alerthub-media

7. Create ConfigMaps with connection strings:
   - postgres-config: PostgreSQL connection URL
   - redis-config: Redis connection URL
   - kafka-config: Kafka bootstrap servers
   - mongodb-config: MongoDB connection URL
   - rabbitmq-config: RabbitMQ connection URL
   - seaweedfs-config: S3 endpoint URL

8. Create Secrets for credentials:
   - postgres-secret: database password
   - mongodb-secret: database password
   - rabbitmq-secret: admin credentials

tasks.description: Build the high-performance core service that receives, validates, and routes notifications with rate limiting and WebSocket support
tasks.details:
1. Initialize Rust project with Axum 0.7, tokio, sqlx, redis:
   cargo init notification-router
   cargo add axum@0.7 tokio@1 sqlx@0.7 redis@0.24 serde@1 serde_json@1 uuid@1 chrono@0.4 validator@0.18 tracing@0.1 prometheus@0.13

2. Define data models in src/models.rs:
   struct Notification { id: Uuid, tenant_id: Uuid, channel: Channel, priority: Priority, payload: NotificationPayload, metadata: HashMap<String, Value>, created_at: DateTime<Utc>, status: NotificationStatus }
   enum Channel { Slack, Discord, Email, Push, Webhook }
   enum Priority { Critical, High, Normal, Low }
   enum NotificationStatus { Pending, Processing, Delivered, Failed { reason: String, attempts: u32 } }

3. Implement database layer with sqlx:
   - Create migration for notifications table
   - Implement NotificationRepository with CRUD operations
   - Add connection pool initialization

4. Implement Redis integration:
   - Rate limiter using token bucket algorithm (redis INCR with TTL)
   - Deduplication cache using notification hash as key
   - Pub/sub for WebSocket broadcasting

5. Build Axum router with endpoints:
   POST /api/v1/notifications - Submit notification (validate, rate limit, dedupe, insert DB, publish Kafka)
   POST /api/v1/notifications/batch - Batch submit (max 100, parallel processing)
   GET /api/v1/notifications/:id - Get status (query DB)
   GET /api/v1/notifications/:id/events - Get delivery events (query DB)
   WS /api/v1/ws - WebSocket connection (subscribe to Redis pub/sub)
   GET /health/live - Liveness check
   GET /health/ready - Readiness check (test DB, Redis, Kafka)
   GET /metrics - Prometheus metrics

6. Implement Kafka producer:
   - Use rdkafka crate
   - Publish to alerthub.notifications.created topic
   - Include notification ID, tenant ID, channel, priority in event

7. Implement WebSocket handler:
   - Maintain connection map (tenant_id -> Vec<WebSocket>)
   - Subscribe to Redis pub/sub channel per tenant
   - Broadcast updates to connected clients

8. Add middleware:
   - JWT authentication (extract tenant_id from token)
   - Request ID generation and propagation
   - Structured logging with tracing
   - Prometheus metrics collection

9. Create Dockerfile:
   FROM rust:1.75 AS builder
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY . .
   RUN cargo build --release
   FROM debian:bookworm-slim
   COPY --from=builder /app/target/release/notification-router /usr/local/bin/
   CMD ["notification-router"]

subtasks.description: Set up the Rust project with Cargo.toml configuration, add all required dependencies (Axum 0.7, tokio, sqlx, redis, etc.), and create the initial project structure with module organization (models, handlers, middleware, db, cache, kafka, websocket).
subtasks.details: Create notification-router project using cargo init. Configure Cargo.toml with all dependencies: axum@0.7, tokio@1 (with full features), sqlx@0.7 (with postgres, runtime-tokio-rustls, migrate), redis@0.24 (with tokio-comp), rdkafka@0.34, serde@1, serde_json@1, uuid@1 (with v4, serde), chrono@0.4, validator@0.18, tracing@0.1, tracing-subscriber@0.3, prometheus@0.13, jsonwebtoken@9, tower@0.4, tower-http@0.5. Create module structure: src/models.rs, src/handlers/, src/middleware/, src/db/, src/cache/, src/kafka/, src/websocket/, src/config.rs, src/error.rs. Set up basic main.rs with tokio runtime initialization.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify project compiles successfully with cargo build. Ensure all dependencies resolve without conflicts.
subtasks.title: Initialize Rust project with dependencies and project structure

subtasks.description: Implement all core data structures including Notification, Channel, Priority, NotificationStatus, NotificationPayload with serde serialization and validator derive macros for input validation.
subtasks.details: In src/models.rs, define: Notification struct with id (Uuid), tenant_id (Uuid), channel (Channel enum), priority (Priority enum), payload (NotificationPayload struct), metadata (HashMap<String, Value>), created_at (DateTime<Utc>), status (NotificationStatus enum). Channel enum: Slack, Discord, Email, Push, Webhook. Priority enum: Critical, High, Normal, Low. NotificationStatus enum: Pending, Processing, Delivered, Failed with reason and attempts. NotificationPayload struct with title, body, and channel-specific fields. Add #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Validate)] to all structs. Implement validation rules: title max 200 chars, body max 2000 chars, tenant_id required. Add request/response DTOs: CreateNotificationRequest, BatchNotificationRequest, NotificationResponse, NotificationEventResponse.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for serialization/deserialization, validation rules, and enum conversions.
subtasks.title: Define data models, enums, and validation schemas

subtasks.description: Create PostgreSQL schema migrations for notifications and events tables, implement NotificationRepository with CRUD operations, and set up connection pool management with health checks.
subtasks.details: Create migrations in migrations/ directory: 001_create_notifications_table.sql (id uuid primary key, tenant_id uuid not null, channel varchar(50), priority varchar(50), payload jsonb, metadata jsonb, created_at timestamptz, status varchar(50), index on tenant_id and created_at), 002_create_notification_events_table.sql (id uuid, notification_id uuid references notifications, event_type varchar(50), timestamp timestamptz, details jsonb). In src/db/repository.rs, implement NotificationRepository struct with methods: create(notification), get_by_id(id), update_status(id, status), get_events(notification_id), batch_create(notifications). In src/db/pool.rs, implement connection pool initialization with sqlx::postgres::PgPoolOptions, configure max connections (50), idle timeout (10min), and health check query. Add database URL configuration from environment.
subtasks.testStrategy: Use sqlx-test or testcontainers-postgres for integration tests. Test CRUD operations, connection pool behavior, and migration execution.
subtasks.title: Implement database layer with sqlx and PostgreSQL migrations

subtasks.description: Build Redis client wrapper with token bucket rate limiter, deduplication cache using notification hashing, and pub/sub channels for WebSocket broadcasting with connection pooling.
subtasks.details: In src/cache/redis_client.rs, create RedisClient struct wrapping redis::aio::ConnectionManager. Implement rate_limit method using token bucket algorithm: INCR key with TTL, compare against limit (e.g., 100 req/min per tenant), return remaining tokens. Implement deduplicate method: compute SHA256 hash of notification payload, SET NX with 5min TTL, return bool if duplicate. In src/cache/pubsub.rs, implement PubSubManager with subscribe/publish methods for tenant-specific channels (format: notifications:tenant:{tenant_id}). Publish notification status updates as JSON. Configure connection pool with max connections (20), connection timeout (5s), and retry logic. Add Redis URL configuration from environment.
subtasks.testStrategy: Use redis testcontainer for integration tests. Test rate limiting under concurrent load, deduplication with identical payloads, and pub/sub message delivery.
subtasks.title: Implement Redis integration for rate limiting, caching, and pub/sub

subtasks.description: Set up rdkafka producer client with configuration for publishing notification events to alerthub.notifications.created topic with proper serialization, error handling, and delivery guarantees.
subtasks.details: In src/kafka/producer.rs, create KafkaProducer struct wrapping FutureProducer from rdkafka. Configure producer with: bootstrap.servers from env, acks=all, retries=3, max.in.flight.requests.per.connection=5, compression.type=snappy, linger.ms=10. Implement publish_notification method that serializes notification event to JSON with fields: id, tenant_id, channel, priority, timestamp, payload_hash. Publish to topic 'alerthub.notifications.created' with tenant_id as partition key for ordering. Implement async error handling with retry logic and dead letter queue logging. Add metrics for publish latency and failures. Create KafkaEvent struct for event schema.
subtasks.testStrategy: Use testcontainers-kafka for integration tests. Verify message delivery, partition key routing, and error handling with broker failures.
subtasks.title: Implement Kafka producer for event publishing

subtasks.description: Implement all REST endpoints including POST /api/v1/notifications, POST /api/v1/notifications/batch, GET /api/v1/notifications/:id, GET /api/v1/notifications/:id/events, and health/metrics endpoints with proper error handling and response formatting.
subtasks.details: In src/handlers/notifications.rs, implement handler functions: create_notification (validate request, check rate limit, deduplicate, insert DB, publish Kafka, return 201 with notification ID), batch_create_notifications (validate max 100, parallel process with tokio::spawn, return batch response with success/failure per item), get_notification (query DB, return 200 or 404), get_notification_events (query events table, return array). In src/handlers/health.rs, implement liveness (return 200 immediately) and readiness (test DB, Redis, Kafka connections, return 200 or 503). In src/handlers/metrics.rs, expose Prometheus metrics. In src/main.rs, create Axum router with routes, apply middleware layers, and start server on 0.0.0.0:8080. Use tower::ServiceBuilder for middleware composition. Add proper error responses with status codes and JSON error bodies.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write integration tests using axum-test or reqwest to test each endpoint. Test validation errors, rate limiting, batch processing, and error scenarios.
subtasks.title: Build Axum router with REST API endpoints

subtasks.description: Build WebSocket endpoint at /api/v1/ws with connection lifecycle management, tenant-based subscription to Redis pub/sub channels, and real-time notification broadcasting to connected clients.
subtasks.details: In src/websocket/handler.rs, implement websocket_handler function that upgrades HTTP to WebSocket using axum::extract::ws. Create ConnectionManager struct with Arc<RwLock<HashMap<Uuid, Vec<WebSocketSender>>>> to track tenant connections. On connection: extract tenant_id from JWT, add to connection map, subscribe to Redis channel notifications:tenant:{tenant_id}. Spawn background task to listen for Redis messages and broadcast to tenant's WebSocket connections. Handle ping/pong for keepalive (30s interval). On disconnect: remove from connection map, unsubscribe from Redis. Implement message serialization for notification updates (JSON format with event_type, notification_id, status). Add connection limits per tenant (max 10 connections). Handle backpressure with bounded channels.
subtasks.testStrategy: Use tungstenite for WebSocket client tests. Test connection establishment, message broadcasting, reconnection handling, and connection limits.
subtasks.title: Implement WebSocket handler with connection management and pub/sub

subtasks.description: Create middleware layers for JWT authentication with tenant extraction, request ID generation and propagation, structured logging with tracing, and Prometheus metrics collection for all endpoints.
subtasks.details: In src/middleware/auth.rs, implement JWT authentication middleware using jsonwebtoken crate. Extract and validate JWT from Authorization header, decode claims to get tenant_id, inject into request extensions. In src/middleware/request_id.rs, generate UUID for each request, add X-Request-ID header to response, store in request extensions. In src/middleware/logging.rs, use tracing-subscriber to log request/response with fields: method, path, status, duration, request_id, tenant_id. Configure JSON formatting for structured logs. In src/middleware/metrics.rs, implement Prometheus metrics: http_requests_total (counter by method, path, status), http_request_duration_seconds (histogram), active_connections (gauge), notification_processing_duration (histogram). Use tower-http::trace for automatic request tracing. Configure middleware order: metrics -> logging -> request_id -> auth -> routes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test JWT validation with valid/invalid/expired tokens. Verify request ID propagation. Check metrics collection and Prometheus endpoint output.
subtasks.title: Implement middleware stack for auth, logging, and metrics

subtasks.description: Build production-ready Dockerfile with multi-stage build process, minimal runtime image, non-root user, and optimized layer caching for fast builds.
subtasks.details: Create Dockerfile with two stages: Builder stage using rust:1.75-slim, copy Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock first for dependency caching, copy source code, run cargo build --release with target-dir optimization. Add .dockerignore with target/, .git/, *.md. Create docker-compose.yml for local development with postgres, redis, kafka services. Add healthcheck in Dockerfile using curl against /health/live. Optimize image size (target <50MB final image).

subtasks.details: In tests/ directory, create: unit tests for models (validation, serialization), repository tests with sqlx-test, Redis client tests with testcontainers-redis, Kafka producer tests with testcontainers-kafka, handler tests with mock dependencies. Create integration_tests.rs using testcontainers-modules for postgres, redis, kafka. Test full request flow: create notification -> verify DB insert -> verify Kafka publish -> verify Redis cache. Add load tests in benches/ using criterion for rate limiting and batch processing (target: 1000 req/s). Create e2e_tests.rs that starts full service and tests all endpoints including WebSocket connections. Use tokio::test for async tests. Add test fixtures for sample notifications. tasks.description: Build the channel delivery service with Effect for type-safe error handling, composable services, and robust retry logic
tasks.details:
1. Initialize Bun project with Elysia and Effect:
   bun init integration-service
   bun add elysia@1 effect@3 @effect/platform @effect/schema drizzle-orm mongodb kafkajs amqplib handlebars

2. Setup Effect services architecture:
   - Define service interfaces using Context.Tag
   - SlackService: deliver(integration, message) => Effect<DeliveryResult, SlackDeliveryError>
   - DiscordService: deliver(integration, message) => Effect<DeliveryResult, DiscordDeliveryError>
   - EmailService: deliver(integration, message) => Effect<DeliveryResult, EmailDeliveryError>
   - WebhookService: deliver(integration, message) => Effect<DeliveryResult, WebhookDeliveryError>
   - PushService: deliver(integration, message) => Effect<DeliveryResult, PushDeliveryError>

3. Define Effect Schemas for validation:
   const Channel = Schema.Literal("slack", "discord", "email", "push", "webhook")
   const Integration = Schema.Struct({ id: Schema.String, tenantId: Schema.String, channel: Channel, name: Schema.String, config: Schema.Union(...), enabled: Schema.Boolean })
   const SlackConfig = Schema.Struct({ webhookUrl: Schema.String, channel: Schema.optional(Schema.String) })
   const DiscordConfig = Schema.Struct({ webhookUrl: Schema.String })
   const EmailConfig = Schema.Struct({ smtpHost: Schema.String, smtpPort: Schema.Number, fromAddress: Schema.String })
   const WebhookConfig = Schema.Struct({ url: Schema.String, headers: Schema.optional(Schema.Record({ key: Schema.String, value: Schema.String })), secret: Schema.optional(Schema.String) })

4. Implement service layers with Effect.Layer:
   const SlackServiceLive = Layer.succeed(SlackService, SlackService.of({
     deliver: (integration, message) => HttpClient.request.post(integration.config.webhookUrl).pipe(
       HttpClient.request.jsonBody(message),
       HttpClient.client.fetchOk,
       Effect.retry(Schedule.exponential("1 second").pipe(Schedule.compose(Schedule.recurs(3)))),
       Effect.mapError((e) => new SlackDeliveryError({ message: e.message }))
     )
   }))

5. Build Elysia API:
   POST /api/v1/integrations - Create integration (validate with Effect Schema)
   GET /api/v1/integrations - List integrations (query MongoDB with Drizzle)
   GET /api/v1/integrations/:id - Get integration
   PATCH /api/v1/integrations/:id - Update integration
   DELETE /api/v1/integrations/:id - Delete integration
   POST /api/v1/integrations/:id/test - Test connectivity (call service.deliver)
   POST /api/v1/integrations/:id/deliver - Manual delivery trigger

6. Implement Kafka consumer with Effect Stream:
   - Connect to Kafka bootstrap servers
   - Subscribe to alerthub.notifications.created topic
   - Transform Kafka messages to Effect Stream
   - Process with Effect.flatMap to route to appropriate service
   - Handle backpressure with Stream.buffer

7. Implement RabbitMQ consumer with Effect Stream:
   - Connect to RabbitMQ
   - Consume from integration.*.delivery queues
   - Route messages to channel services
   - Acknowledge on success, nack on failure (requeue with limit)

8. Implement template rendering:
   - Store templates in MongoDB
   - Use Handlebars for rendering
   - Wrap in Effect for error handling

9. Add rate limiting per channel:
   - Use Effect.Semaphore for concurrency control
   - Configure per-channel limits (Slack: 1 req/sec, Discord: 5 req/sec, etc.)

10. Create Dockerfile:
   FROM oven/bun:1.1
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
   RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
   COPY . .
   CMD ["bun", "run", "src/index.ts"]

subtasks.description: Set up the foundational Bun project structure with all required dependencies including Elysia for HTTP server, Effect for functional programming, database drivers, message queue clients, and template engine.

Install dependencies: 'bun add elysia@1 effect@3 @effect/platform @effect/schema drizzle-orm mongodb kafkajs amqplib handlebars'. Create src/ directory structure with folders for services/, schemas/, api/, consumers/, and utils/. Set up tsconfig.json with strict mode and Effect-compatible settings. Create basic index.ts entry point with Elysia server initialization. Add .env.example with configuration variables for MongoDB, Kafka, RabbitMQ, and service ports.

subtasks.description: Create type-safe service interfaces using Effect's Context.Tag pattern for Slack, Discord, Email, Webhook, and Push services with standardized delivery signatures.
subtasks.details: In src/services/interfaces.ts, define Context.Tag for each service: SlackService, DiscordService, EmailService, WebhookService, PushService. Each service interface should have a 'deliver' method signature: (integration: Integration, message: Message) => Effect<DeliveryResult, ChannelDeliveryError>. Create base types for DeliveryResult (success: boolean, messageId: string, timestamp: Date, metadata: Record<string, unknown>) and define specific error types for each channel (SlackDeliveryError, DiscordDeliveryError, etc.) extending a base ChannelDeliveryError class with tagged unions for error discrimination. Export all service tags and types from a central services/index.ts file.

Map HTTP errors to SlackDeliveryError with contextual information (status code, response body). Handle rate limiting (429) with backoff. Similarly, implement DiscordServiceLive in src/services/discord.ts with Discord-specific message formatting (embeds, content). In src/services/push.ts, implement PushServiceLive with provider abstraction (FCM, APNs, OneSignal) using strategy pattern. Each provider should have its own Effect-based implementation. Subscribe to 'alerthub.notifications.created' topic with consumer group 'integration-service'. Transform Kafka messages to Effect Stream using Stream.fromAsyncIterable. For each message: parse JSON payload, validate against Message schema, lookup active integrations for tenant/channel from MongoDB, call appropriate service.deliver for each integration. Use Stream.mapEffect for parallel processing with bounded concurrency. Implement backpressure with Stream.buffer(100). Handle deserialization errors gracefully (log and skip). Commit offsets only after successful delivery. Add metrics for messages processed, delivery success/failure rates. Implement graceful shutdown that drains in-flight messages.

subtasks.description: Build RabbitMQ consumer that processes messages from integration delivery queues, routes to channel services, and handles acknowledgments with requeue logic.
subtasks.details: In src/consumers/rabbitmq.ts, create RabbitMQConsumer using amqplib wrapped in Effect. Connect to RabbitMQ server from config. Declare and consume from queues: 'integration.slack.delivery', 'integration.discord.delivery', 'integration.email.delivery', 'integration.webhook.delivery', 'integration.push.delivery'. Transform messages to Effect Stream. Parse message payload (integrationId, message data), load integration from MongoDB, call appropriate service.deliver. On success: ack message. On failure: check retry count in message headers, if under limit (3 retries) nack with requeue and increment retry count, else send to dead letter queue. Use Stream.mapEffectPar for concurrent processing (10 concurrent messages per queue). Add prefetch limit (20). Implement queue health monitoring and circuit breaker for repeated failures.

tasks.description: Build the management API for tenants, users, rules, and analytics using gRPC with REST gateway
tasks.details:
1. Initialize Go project with gRPC:
   go mod init github.com/alerthub/admin-api
   go get google.golang.org/grpc google.golang.org/protobuf github.com/grpc-ecosystem/grpc-gateway/v2 github.com/jackc/pgx/v5 github.com/redis/go-redis/v9

2. Define protobuf schemas in api/proto/admin.proto:
   service TenantService { rpc CreateTenant, GetTenant, UpdateTenant, ListTenants, DeleteTenant }
   service UserService { rpc CreateUser, GetUser, UpdateUser, ListUsers, DeleteUser, UpdatePreferences }
   service RuleService { rpc CreateRule, GetRule, UpdateRule, ListRules, DeleteRule, EvaluateRule }
   service AnalyticsService { rpc GetNotificationStats, GetDeliveryMetrics, GetChannelBreakdown }
   message Tenant { string id, string name, string plan, TenantSettings settings, google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at }
   message User { string id, string tenant_id, string email, string role, UserPreferences preferences }
   message NotificationRule { string id, string tenant_id, string name, repeated RuleCondition conditions, repeated RuleAction actions, bool enabled, int32 priority }

3. Generate Go code and gRPC gateway:
   protoc --go_out=. --go-grpc_out=. --grpc-gateway_out=. api/proto/admin.proto

4. Implement database layer with pgx:
   - Create migrations for tenants, users, rules, audit_logs tables
   - Implement repository pattern for each entity
   - Add connection pool with pgxpool

5. Implement gRPC service handlers:
   type TenantServiceServer struct { db *pgxpool.Pool, redis *redis.Client }
   func (s *TenantServiceServer) CreateTenant(ctx context.Context, req *CreateTenantRequest) (*Tenant, error) {
     // Validate request, insert to DB, return tenant
   }
   // Implement all other RPC methods

6. Implement authentication middleware:
   - JWT validation using RS256
   - Extract tenant_id and user_id from token
   - Add to context for downstream handlers

7. Implement RBAC authorization:
   - Define role permissions (owner: all, admin: CRUD, member: read/create, viewer: read)
   - Check permissions in each RPC handler
   - Return PermissionDenied error if unauthorized

8. Implement notification rules engine:
   - Parse rule conditions (field, operator, value)
   - Evaluate against notification metadata
   - Support operators: eq, ne, gt, lt, contains, regex
   - Return matching actions

9. Implement analytics aggregation:
   - Query notifications table with time range filters
   - Aggregate by status, channel, priority
   - Cache results in Redis with TTL
   - Return NotificationStats and DeliveryMetrics

10. Setup grpc-gateway for REST:
   - Register HTTP handlers from generated code
   - Map gRPC endpoints to REST paths
   - Add CORS middleware

11. Add audit logging:
   - Log all write operations (create, update, delete)
   - Include user_id, tenant_id, action, timestamp, changes
   - Store in audit_logs table

12. Create Dockerfile:
   FROM golang:1.22 AS builder
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY go.mod go.sum ./
   RUN go mod download
   COPY . .
   RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o admin-api cmd/server/main.go
   FROM alpine:latest
   COPY --from=builder /app/admin-api /usr/local/bin/
   CMD ["admin-api"]

tasks.description: Build the primary web interface for AlertHub with Effect for type-safe data fetching, validation, and error handling
tasks.details:
1. Initialize Next.js 15 project with App Router:
   npx create-next-app@latest web-console --typescript --tailwind --app
   cd web-console
   bun add effect @effect/schema @tanstack/react-query react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers recharts anime.js
   bunx shadcn@latest init
   bunx shadcn@latest add button card form input select table toast

2. Setup Effect Schemas for API responses:
   const NotificationSchema = Schema.Struct({ id: Schema.String, status: Schema.Literal("pending", "processing", "delivered", "failed"), channel: Schema.Literal("slack", "discord", "email", "push", "webhook"), priority: Schema.Literal("critical", "high", "normal", "low"), payload: Schema.Struct({ title: Schema.String, body: Schema.String }), createdAt: Schema.Date })
   const IntegrationSchema = Schema.Struct({ id: Schema.String, name: Schema.String, channel: Schema.Literal(...), enabled: Schema.Boolean })
   const RuleSchema = Schema.Struct({ id: Schema.String, name: Schema.String, conditions: Schema.Array(...), actions: Schema.Array(...), enabled: Schema.Boolean })

3. Implement Effect-powered API client:
   const fetchNotifications = Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => fetch("/api/notifications").then(r => r.json()), catch: () => new ApiError({ message: "Failed to fetch" }) }).pipe(Effect.flatMap(Schema.decodeUnknown(Schema.Array(NotificationSchema))))
   const createIntegration = (data: CreateIntegration) => Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => fetch("/api/integrations", { method: "POST", body: JSON.stringify(data) }).then(r => r.json()), catch: () => new ApiError({ message: "Failed to create" }) }).pipe(Effect.flatMap(Schema.decodeUnknown(IntegrationSchema)))

4. Create TanStack Query hooks with Effect:
   function useNotifications() { return useQuery({ queryKey: ["notifications"], queryFn: () => Effect.runPromise(fetchNotifications) }) }
   function useCreateIntegration() { return useMutation({ mutationFn: (data) => Effect.runPromise(createIntegration(data)) }) }

5. Build page components:
   app/page.tsx - Dashboard with notification stats, recent activity, quick actions
   app/notifications/page.tsx - Notification history with filters (status, channel, date range), pagination, search
   app/integrations/page.tsx - Integration cards with status, actions (test, edit, delete), add new button
   app/rules/page.tsx - Rule list with drag-and-drop priority ordering, create/edit modal
   app/settings/page.tsx - Tenant settings, user preferences, API keys
   app/analytics/page.tsx - Charts for delivery metrics (success rate, channel breakdown, time series)

6. Implement NotificationFeed component with WebSocket:
   const notificationStream = Stream.async<Notification, WebSocketError>((emit) => {
     const ws = new WebSocket("/api/v1/ws")
     ws.onmessage = (event) => emit.single(Schema.decodeUnknownSync(NotificationSchema)(JSON.parse(event.data)))
     ws.onerror = () => emit.fail(new WebSocketError({ message: "Connection failed" }))
     return Effect.sync(() => ws.close())
   })
   Use Effect.Stream.runForEach to update UI state

7. Implement form validation with Effect Schema:
   const CreateIntegrationSchema = Schema.Struct({ name: Schema.String.pipe(Schema.minLength(1)), channel: Schema.Literal(...), webhookUrl: Schema.optional(Schema.String.pipe(Schema.pattern(/^https?:\/\//))), enabled: Schema.Boolean })
   const form = useForm({ resolver: effectResolver(CreateIntegrationSchema) })

8. Build RuleBuilder component:
   - Drag-and-drop interface for conditions (field, operator, value)
   - Visual action configuration (route to channel, set priority, etc.)
   - Preview rule evaluation with sample data
   - Save/update with Effect error handling

9. Implement AnalyticsChart component:
   - Use recharts for line/bar/pie charts
   - Fetch data with Effect and TanStack Query
   - Transform data with Effect.map
   - Add date range selector, refresh button

10. Add dark/light theme:
   - Use next-themes for theme switching
   - Configure TailwindCSS dark mode
   - Add theme toggle in header

11. Implement toast notifications:
   - Use shadcn toast component
   - Map Effect errors to user-friendly messages
   - Show success toasts for actions

12. Create Dockerfile:
   FROM oven/bun:1.1 AS builder
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
   RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
   COPY . .
   RUN bun run build
   FROM oven/bun:1.1
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY --from=builder /app/.next ./.next
   COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
   COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./
   CMD ["bun", "run", "start"]

subtasks.details: Create hooks/useNotifications.ts with useNotifications(filters), useNotification(id), useCreateNotification(), useUpdateNotification(). Create hooks/useIntegrations.ts with useIntegrations(), useIntegration(id), useCreateIntegration(), useUpdateIntegration(), useDeleteIntegration(). Create hooks/useRules.ts with useRules(), useRule(id), useCreateRule(), useUpdateRule(), useDeleteRule(), useReorderRules(). Create hooks/useAnalytics.ts with useAnalytics(dateRange). Create hooks/useSettings.ts with useSettings(), useUpdateSettings(). Each hook should wrap Effect.runPromise in queryFn/mutationFn, handle loading/error states, implement optimistic updates for mutations, and provide proper TypeScript types.

subtasks.description: Build the main Dashboard page with stats/metrics and the Notifications page with filtering, search, and pagination capabilities.

Create app/notifications/page.tsx with: NotificationTable component using useNotifications with server-side pagination, FilterBar with status/channel/date range filters using React Hook Form, SearchInput with debounced search, NotificationDetail modal showing full payload. Use shadcn/ui Table, Select, Input components. Implement URL state management for filters with useSearchParams. Add loading skeletons and empty states.
subtasks.testStrategy: Component tests with React Testing Library for user interactions, test filter changes update URL params, verify pagination works, test search debouncing, check loading/error states render correctly

subtasks.description: Create the Integrations management page with full CRUD operations and the Settings page for tenant/user configuration.
subtasks.details: Create app/integrations/page.tsx with: IntegrationGrid displaying cards for each integration using useIntegrations, IntegrationCard showing name/channel/status/lastSync with actions (test, edit, delete), AddIntegrationDialog with form using CreateIntegrationSchema validation, EditIntegrationDialog for updates, TestIntegrationButton triggering test endpoint. Create app/settings/page.tsx with: TenantSettingsForm for organization config, UserPreferencesForm for notification preferences, ApiKeysSection for generating/revoking keys, ThemeSelector for dark/light mode. Use React Hook Form with effectResolver for all forms. Implement toast notifications for success/error feedback.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test form validation with invalid inputs, verify CRUD operations trigger correct API calls, test toast notifications appear, check dialog open/close behavior, validate optimistic updates work
subtasks.title: Build Integrations and Settings pages with CRUD operations

subtasks.description: Implement the Rules management page with an interactive visual rule builder supporting drag-and-drop condition/action configuration.
subtasks.details: Create app/rules/page.tsx with: RuleList showing all rules with useRules, drag-and-drop reordering using @dnd-kit/core for priority management, RuleCard displaying name/status/condition count. Create components/features/RuleBuilder.tsx with: ConditionBuilder allowing drag-and-drop of condition blocks (field selector, operator dropdown, value input), ActionBuilder for configuring actions (route to channel, set priority, transform payload), RulePreview showing JSON representation, TestRuleSection to evaluate rule against sample data. Create CreateRuleDialog and EditRuleDialog with RuleBuilder embedded. Implement useReorderRules mutation for priority updates. Use RuleSchema for validation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test drag-and-drop reordering updates priority correctly, verify rule builder validates conditions/actions, test rule preview updates in real-time, check rule evaluation with sample data, validate form submission

subtasks.description: Create the Analytics dashboard with Recharts visualizations for delivery metrics, success rates, channel breakdowns, and time series data.
subtasks.details: Create app/analytics/page.tsx with: DateRangePicker for selecting time period, MetricsOverview showing KPIs (total notifications, success rate, avg delivery time), DeliverySuccessChart (line chart) showing success/failure over time using recharts LineChart, ChannelBreakdownChart (pie chart) showing distribution by channel using recharts PieChart, PriorityDistributionChart (bar chart) using recharts BarChart, StatusTimelineChart showing status transitions. Create components/features/AnalyticsChart.tsx wrapper handling data transformation with Effect.map. Implement useAnalytics hook with date range parameter. Add export functionality for chart data. Use responsive design for charts.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test chart renders with mock data, verify date range filter updates charts, test responsive behavior on mobile, check data transformation logic, validate export functionality
subtasks.title: Build Analytics page with interactive charts and metrics

subtasks.description: Build real-time notification feed using WebSocket connection managed by Effect Stream with automatic reconnection and error handling.
subtasks.details: Create lib/websocket/notificationStream.ts with: createNotificationStream() returning Effect.Stream that connects to /api/v1/ws, handles WebSocket lifecycle (open, message, error, close), parses messages with NotificationSchema, implements exponential backoff reconnection with Effect.retry. Create hooks/useNotificationStream.ts wrapping Stream with React state: useEffect to run Effect.Stream.runForEach, update local state on new messages, cleanup on unmount. Create components/features/NotificationFeed.tsx displaying live notifications with animations using anime.js. Implement toast notifications for critical alerts. Add connection status indicator. Handle authentication in WebSocket connection.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock WebSocket server for testing, verify stream processes messages correctly, test reconnection logic on disconnect, check memory leaks on unmount, validate authentication works
subtasks.title: Implement WebSocket integration with Effect Stream for real-time updates

Update tailwind.config.ts for dark mode class strategy with custom color variables. Implement skeleton loaders for all data-fetching components. Add micro-interactions (button press, form focus). Ensure WCAG AA contrast ratios in both themes.

tasks.description: Build the mobile application for iOS and Android with push notification support and offline caching
tasks.details:
1. Initialize Expo project:
   npx create-expo-app mobile-app --template blank-typescript
   cd mobile-app
   npx expo install expo-notifications expo-secure-store expo-router nativewind react-native-reanimated @tanstack/react-query

2. Setup NativeWind for styling:
   - Configure tailwind.config.js
   - Add NativeWind provider to app/_layout.tsx

3. Configure Expo Router file-based navigation:
   app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx - Bottom tab navigator
   app/(tabs)/index.tsx - Home screen (notifications feed)
   app/(tabs)/integrations.tsx - Integrations list
   app/(tabs)/settings.tsx - Settings screen
   app/(tabs)/profile.tsx - Profile screen
   app/notification/[id].tsx - Notification detail screen

4. Implement push notification registration:
   - Request permissions with expo-notifications
   - Register device token with FCM
   - Send token to backend API
   - Handle notification received (foreground/background)
   - Handle notification tapped (deep link to detail screen)

5. Build HomeScreen component:
   - Fetch notifications with TanStack Query
   - Display in FlatList with pull-to-refresh
   - Show notification card (title, body, timestamp, status badge)
   - Tap to navigate to detail screen
   - Swipe actions (mark as read, delete)

6. Build NotificationDetailScreen:
   - Display full notification with metadata
   - Show delivery status and events
   - Action buttons (retry, dismiss)
   - Share functionality

7. Build IntegrationsScreen:
   - List connected integrations
   - Show status indicator (enabled/disabled)
   - Tap to view integration details
   - No edit functionality (web-only)

8. Build SettingsScreen:
   - Notification preferences (enable/disable channels)
   - Quiet hours configuration
   - Biometric authentication toggle
   - Theme selection (light/dark/system)
   - Logout button

9. Build ProfileScreen:
   - Display user info (name, email, tenant)
   - Account statistics (total notifications, delivery rate)
   - App version and build number

10. Implement offline caching:
   - Use TanStack Query with persistQueryClient
   - Store notifications in AsyncStorage
   - Show cached data when offline
   - Sync on reconnection

11. Implement biometric authentication:
   - Use expo-local-authentication
   - Prompt for Face ID/fingerprint on app launch
   - Store auth state in expo-secure-store

12. Configure app.json:
   - Set app name, slug, version
   - Configure notification settings (icon, sound, badge)
   - Set iOS bundle ID and Android package
   - Configure deep linking scheme (alerthub://)

13. Build APK/IPA:
   - Configure EAS Build
   - Create build profiles (development, preview, production)
   - Run eas build --platform all

subtasks.description: Create a new Expo project with TypeScript template and install all required dependencies including expo-notifications, expo-secure-store, expo-router, nativewind, react-native-reanimated, and @tanstack/react-query. Configure NativeWind for styling and set up the basic project structure.
subtasks.details: Run 'npx create-expo-app mobile-app --template blank-typescript' to initialize the project. Install dependencies: expo-notifications, expo-secure-store, expo-router, nativewind, react-native-reanimated, @tanstack/react-query, expo-local-authentication, and AsyncStorage. Configure tailwind.config.js for NativeWind. Set up the NativeWind provider in app/_layout.tsx. Create the basic folder structure for app/(tabs) and app/notification directories. Configure babel.config.js for react-native-reanimated and nativewind. Ensure TypeScript is properly configured with strict mode enabled.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify project builds successfully with 'npx expo start'. Check that all dependencies are installed without conflicts. Validate TypeScript compilation works without errors.
subtasks.title: Initialize Expo project with TypeScript and core dependencies

subtasks.description: Set up file-based navigation using Expo Router with bottom tab navigator and all required screens including home, integrations, settings, profile, and notification detail screens.
subtasks.details: Create app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx with bottom tab navigator configuration using Expo Router. Implement tab screens: app/(tabs)/index.tsx (Home/notifications feed), app/(tabs)/integrations.tsx (Integrations list), app/(tabs)/settings.tsx (Settings), app/(tabs)/profile.tsx (Profile). Create app/notification/[id].tsx for notification detail screen with dynamic routing. Configure tab icons and labels. Set up deep linking scheme 'alerthub://' in app.json. Implement navigation types for TypeScript safety. Add navigation guards and error boundaries for each route.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test navigation between all tabs. Verify deep linking works with test URLs. Check that dynamic routes handle parameters correctly. Validate back navigation behavior.
subtasks.title: Implement Expo Router navigation structure with tab layout

subtasks.description: Build the complete push notification system including permission requests, FCM/APNs registration, device token management, and notification event handlers for foreground, background, and tap interactions.
subtasks.details: Use expo-notifications to request permissions on app launch. Implement device token registration with FCM for Android and APNs for iOS. Create API service to send device token to backend. Set up notification listeners for received notifications (foreground and background states). Implement notification tap handler that deep links to notification detail screen using expo-router. Configure notification channel settings for Android. Handle notification badge count updates. Store notification preferences in expo-secure-store. Implement retry logic for failed token registration. Add error handling for permission denial scenarios.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test permission request flow on both iOS and Android. Verify device token is successfully sent to backend. Test notification display in foreground and background. Validate deep linking from notification tap opens correct screen with notification ID.
subtasks.title: Implement push notification registration and handling system

subtasks.description: Create the main home screen displaying notifications in a scrollable list with pull-to-refresh, swipe actions, and real-time updates using TanStack Query for data fetching and caching.
subtasks.details: Implement HomeScreen component using FlatList to display notifications. Integrate TanStack Query for fetching notifications from backend API with automatic refetching and caching. Add pull-to-refresh functionality with RefreshControl. Create NotificationCard component showing title, body, timestamp, and status badge with appropriate styling. Implement swipe actions using react-native-gesture-handler for mark as read and delete operations. Add tap handler to navigate to notification detail screen. Implement empty state UI for when no notifications exist. Add loading skeleton while fetching. Handle pagination with infinite scroll if needed. Use NativeWind for responsive styling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test notification list renders correctly with mock data. Verify pull-to-refresh triggers data refetch. Test swipe actions update notification status. Validate navigation to detail screen passes correct notification ID. Check loading and empty states display properly.
subtasks.title: Build Home screen with notification feed and interactive features

subtasks.description: Implement the remaining four screens: notification detail with full metadata and actions, integrations list (read-only), settings with preferences and biometric toggle, and profile with user statistics.
subtasks.details: NotificationDetailScreen: Display full notification content, metadata, delivery status, and event timeline. Add action buttons for retry and dismiss. Implement share functionality using expo-sharing. IntegrationsScreen: Fetch and display list of connected integrations with status indicators (enabled/disabled). Show integration icons and names. Add tap handler to view details (read-only). SettingsScreen: Create sections for notification preferences (channel enable/disable), quiet hours configuration with time pickers, biometric authentication toggle, theme selection (light/dark/system), and logout button. ProfileScreen: Display user information (name, email, tenant), account statistics (total notifications sent, delivery rate), and app version/build number from app.json. Use TanStack Query for data fetching. Apply consistent styling with NativeWind across all screens.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each screen independently with mock data. Verify NotificationDetail displays all metadata correctly. Test share functionality. Validate Settings toggles update preferences. Check Profile displays accurate statistics. Test theme switching applies correctly.
subtasks.title: Build Notification Detail, Integrations, Settings, and Profile screens

subtasks.description: Set up comprehensive offline support using TanStack Query with AsyncStorage persistence, enabling the app to display cached data when offline and sync automatically on reconnection.
subtasks.details: Configure TanStack Query with persistQueryClient using AsyncStorage as the storage mechanism. Set up appropriate cache times and stale times for different query types. Implement network status detection using NetInfo. Create offline indicator UI component to show connection status. Configure automatic background refetching when connection is restored. Set up optimistic updates for mutations (mark as read, delete) that queue when offline and sync on reconnection. Implement conflict resolution strategy for offline changes. Add cache invalidation logic. Store critical data like user preferences and authentication tokens in expo-secure-store for secure persistence. Create utility functions for cache management and cleanup.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test app functionality in airplane mode with cached data. Verify data persists across app restarts. Test automatic sync when connection is restored. Validate optimistic updates queue and execute correctly. Check offline indicator displays appropriately.
subtasks.title: Implement offline caching with TanStack Query persistence

subtasks.description: Add biometric authentication (Face ID/Touch ID/Fingerprint) that prompts users on app launch and securely stores authentication state using expo-secure-store.
subtasks.details: Use expo-local-authentication to check device biometric capabilities on app launch. Implement authentication prompt with appropriate fallback for devices without biometric support. Create authentication context/provider to manage auth state across the app. Store authentication state and user session tokens securely in expo-secure-store. Implement auto-lock functionality that requires re-authentication after app backgrounding for specified duration. Add settings toggle to enable/disable biometric authentication. Handle authentication failures with retry logic and fallback to manual login. Implement session timeout management. Add biometric authentication for sensitive actions like viewing notification details or accessing settings.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test biometric prompt appears on app launch when enabled. Verify successful authentication grants access. Test fallback authentication method works. Validate authentication state persists correctly. Check auto-lock triggers re-authentication after timeout.
subtasks.title: Implement biometric authentication with expo-local-authentication

subtasks.description: Conduct comprehensive code review of all implemented components, screens, and services to ensure code quality, proper TypeScript usage, React Native best practices, accessibility, and consistent patterns.
subtasks.details: Review all React Native components for proper hooks usage, performance optimization (useMemo, useCallback), and avoiding unnecessary re-renders. Check TypeScript types are properly defined with no 'any' types. Verify proper error handling and loading states throughout the app. Review navigation implementation for correct patterns and type safety. Check TanStack Query usage follows best practices with proper query keys and cache management. Verify offline caching implementation is robust. Review biometric authentication flow for security best practices. Check accessibility features (screen reader support, proper labels, contrast ratios). subtasks.description: Set up EAS Build configuration with build profiles for development, preview, and production. Configure app.json with all required settings and generate builds for both iOS and Android platforms.

Configure app.json with complete settings: app name, slug, version, iOS bundle identifier, Android package name, notification settings (icon, sound, badge, channels), deep linking scheme (alerthub://), permissions (notifications, biometric), splash screen, and app icons for both platforms. Create .easignore file to exclude unnecessary files from builds. Run 'eas build --platform all --profile preview' to generate builds for testing. Document build process and submission steps for App Store and Google Play. Create build scripts for CI/CD integration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify builds complete successfully for both platforms. Test development build on physical devices. Validate preview builds install and run correctly. Check all app.json configurations are applied (icons, splash screen, permissions). Test deep linking works in production builds.
subtasks.title: Configure EAS Build and create deployment builds for iOS and Android

tasks.description: Build the cross-platform desktop application with system tray integration and native notifications
tasks.details:
1. Initialize Electron project:
   npm init -y
   npm install electron electron-builder electron-updater react react-dom
   npm install -D @types/react @types/react-dom typescript webpack webpack-cli

2. Setup project structure:
   src/main/main.ts - Electron main process
   src/renderer/App.tsx - React app
   src/preload/preload.ts - Preload script (IPC bridge)
   public/ - Static assets (icons, HTML)

3. Implement main process (src/main/main.ts):
   - Create BrowserWindow with preload script
   - Setup system tray with icon and menu
   - Register global shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+A to show/hide)
   - Handle app lifecycle (ready, window-all-closed, activate)
   - Setup auto-updater with electron-updater
   - Configure auto-launch on system boot

4. Implement system tray:
   - Create tray icon with unread count badge
   - Build context menu:
     * Show/Hide main window
     * Recent notifications (last 5)
     * Mute for 1 hour / until tomorrow
     * Preferences
     * Quit
   - Update badge count on notification received

5. Implement IPC communication:
   - main -> renderer: notification-received, settings-updated
   - renderer -> main: show-notification, update-settings, quit-app
   - Use contextBridge in preload script for secure IPC

6. Build renderer React app:
   - MainWindow: Full notification feed with filters
   - MiniWindow: Quick view popup (200x400px)
   - SettingsWindow: Preferences and account settings

7. Implement MainWindow component:
   - Notification list with virtual scrolling
   - Filters (status, channel, date range)
   - Search bar
   - Action buttons (mark as read, delete, refresh)
   - WebSocket connection for real-time updates

8. Implement MiniWindow component:
   - Compact notification cards (last 10)
   - Quick actions (dismiss, view details)
   - Click to open MainWindow with selected notification

9. Implement SettingsWindow component:
   - Notification preferences (enable/disable channels)
   - Desktop notification settings (sound, position)
   - Startup options (launch on boot, start minimized)
   - Account info and logout

10. Implement native desktop notifications:
   - Use Electron Notification API
   - Show notification with title, body, icon
   - Handle notification click (focus window, navigate to detail)
   - Respect user preferences (quiet hours, mute)

11. Configure electron-builder:
   - Set app name, product name, app ID
   - Configure build targets (Windows NSIS, macOS DMG, Linux AppImage)
   - Set icon paths for each platform
   - Configure auto-update settings

12. Create Dockerfile for build environment:
   FROM electronuserland/builder:wine
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
   RUN npm ci
   COPY . .
   RUN npm run build
   CMD ["npm", "run", "dist"]

subtasks.description: Set up the foundational Electron project structure with all necessary dependencies, TypeScript configuration, webpack setup, and folder structure for main/renderer/preload processes.
subtasks.details: Initialize npm project and install core dependencies: electron, electron-builder, electron-updater, react, react-dom, typescript, webpack, and all necessary type definitions. Create tsconfig.json for both main and renderer processes. Set up webpack configuration for renderer bundling. Create folder structure: src/main/, src/renderer/, src/preload/, public/. Add build scripts to package.json for development and production. Configure ESLint and Prettier for code quality.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify project builds successfully with 'npm run build' and Electron launches with 'npm start'
subtasks.title: Initialize Electron project with React, TypeScript, and build tooling

subtasks.description: Build the main process (main.ts) that manages application lifecycle, creates browser windows, and handles app-level events.
subtasks.details: Create src/main/main.ts with BrowserWindow creation using preload script for security. Implement app lifecycle handlers: 'ready', 'window-all-closed', 'activate' (macOS behavior). Configure BrowserWindow with proper security settings (nodeIntegration: false, contextIsolation: true). Set up window state management (remember size/position). Implement show/hide window functionality. Add proper cleanup on app quit. Configure Content Security Policy headers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual testing of window creation, minimize/maximize, close behavior, and app lifecycle on all platforms
subtasks.title: Implement Electron main process with window management and lifecycle

subtasks.description: Create system tray icon with unread notification badge, context menu with actions, and tray click handlers for quick access.
subtasks.details: Implement Tray class in main process with platform-specific icons (16x16 for Windows, 22x22 for macOS). Build context menu with: Show/Hide window, Recent notifications (last 5 with click handlers), Mute options (1 hour/until tomorrow), Preferences, Quit. Implement dynamic badge overlay showing unread count (1-99+). Handle tray icon click to toggle window visibility. Update tray tooltip with unread count. Support both light/dark mode icons for macOS. Implement notification count state management.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test tray icon visibility, menu interactions, badge updates, and platform-specific behaviors on Windows, macOS, and Linux
subtasks.title: Implement system tray integration with dynamic badge and context menu

subtasks.description: Set up bidirectional IPC communication between main and renderer processes using contextBridge for security, exposing only necessary APIs to renderer.
subtasks.details: Create src/preload/preload.ts with contextBridge to expose safe IPC methods. Implement main->renderer channels: 'notification-received', 'settings-updated', 'tray-click', 'update-available'. Implement renderer->main channels: 'show-notification', 'update-settings', 'mark-as-read', 'quit-app', 'open-external-link'. Use ipcMain.handle for request/response patterns and ipcMain.on for fire-and-forget. Add TypeScript definitions for exposed API in renderer. Implement error handling and validation for all IPC messages. Follow principle of least privilege.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for IPC message validation and integration tests for main<->renderer communication flows
subtasks.title: Implement secure IPC communication with contextBridge and preload script

subtasks.description: Create the primary React application window with full notification list, filtering, search, and WebSocket integration for live updates.
subtasks.details: Create src/renderer/App.tsx with React Router for multi-window support. Implement MainWindow component with virtual scrolling (react-window) for performance with large lists. Add notification list with cards showing title, body, timestamp, channel, status. Implement filters: status (unread/read/all), channel selection, date range picker. Add search bar with debounced API calls. Implement action buttons: mark as read/unread, delete, refresh, mark all as read. Set up WebSocket connection to backend for real-time notification push. Handle connection states (connecting, connected, disconnected). Implement optimistic UI updates. Add loading states and error handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Component testing with React Testing Library, manual testing of real-time updates and filter interactions
subtasks.title: Build MainWindow React component with notification feed and real-time updates

subtasks.description: Create compact popup window for quick notification viewing and settings window for user preferences and account management.
subtasks.details: Build MiniWindow component (200x400px) showing last 10 notifications in compact cards with quick actions (dismiss, view details). Implement click handler to open MainWindow with selected notification focused. Add auto-hide on focus loss option. Build SettingsWindow component with tabs: Notifications (enable/disable per channel, sound preferences, notification position), Appearance (theme, font size), Startup (launch on boot, start minimized, show in dock), Account (user info, logout button). Implement form validation and settings persistence via IPC. Add keyboard shortcuts for navigation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Component testing for both windows, manual testing of settings persistence and MiniWindow behavior
subtasks.title: Implement MiniWindow compact view and SettingsWindow components

subtasks.description: Create native notification system using Electron's Notification API with click handlers, sound options, and user preference respect.
subtasks.details: Implement notification manager in main process using Electron Notification API. Create notifications with title, body, icon, urgency level. Handle notification click to focus window and navigate to notification detail. Implement notification sound with user preference (on/off/custom). Add quiet hours functionality (check time before showing). Implement mute state (1 hour, until tomorrow, indefinitely). Request notification permissions on first launch. Handle platform-specific notification behaviors (Windows Action Center, macOS Notification Center, Linux notify-send). Implement notification grouping for multiple notifications. Add notification action buttons where supported.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual testing of notifications on all platforms, verify click handlers, sound playback, and quiet hours functionality
subtasks.title: Implement native desktop notifications with Electron Notification API

subtasks.description: Add keyboard shortcuts for quick access, auto-launch on system boot capability, and automatic update checking with electron-updater.
subtasks.details: Register global shortcuts: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+A to show/hide main window, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+M for MiniWindow. Implement auto-launch using electron's app.setLoginItemSettings for macOS/Windows and .desktop file for Linux. Add toggle in settings. tasks.description: Create Kubernetes manifests and deploy the Notification Router service to the cluster

   FROM rust:1.75 AS builder
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY Cargo.toml Cargo.lock ./
   COPY src ./src
   RUN cargo build --release
   FROM debian:bookworm-slim
   RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
   COPY --from=builder /app/target/release/notification-router /usr/local/bin/
   EXPOSE 8080
   CMD ["notification-router"]

tasks.description: Build production APK/IPA and prepare for app store submission
tasks.details:
1. Configure EAS Build:
   - Install EAS CLI: npm install -g eas-cli
   - Login: eas login
   - Configure project: eas build:configure

2. Update app.json for production:
   - Set version and buildNumber
   - Configure app icons (1024x1024 for iOS, adaptive icon for Android)
   - Set splash screen
   - Configure deep linking scheme (alerthub://)
   - Set iOS bundle ID and Android package
   - Configure push notification credentials (FCM server key, APNs key)

3. Setup environment variables:
   - Create eas.json with production profile
   - Add API_URL, WS_URL environment variables
   - Configure secrets (API keys) in EAS

4. Build iOS app:
   - eas build --platform ios --profile production
   - Download IPA from EAS dashboard

5. Build Android app:
   - eas build --platform android --profile production
   - Download APK/AAB from EAS dashboard

6. Prepare App Store submission (iOS):
   - Create app in App Store Connect
   - Upload screenshots (6.5", 5.5" iPhone, 12.9" iPad)
   - Write app description, keywords, release notes
   - Set age rating, privacy policy URL
   - Upload IPA via Transporter or EAS Submit

7. Prepare Google Play submission (Android):
   - Create app in Google Play Console
   - Upload screenshots (phone, tablet, 7", 10")
   - Write app description, short description, release notes
   - Set content rating, privacy policy URL
   - Upload AAB via Google Play Console or EAS Submit

8. Configure push notifications:
   - Upload APNs key to EAS
   - Configure FCM server key in EAS
   - Test push notifications on TestFlight/internal testing

9. Submit for review:
   - iOS: Submit to App Store review
   - Android: Submit to Google Play review

tasks.description: Build production installers for Windows, macOS, and Linux and setup auto-update
tasks.details:
1. Configure electron-builder in package.json:
   "build": {
     "appId": "com.alerthub.desktop",
     "productName": "AlertHub",
     "directories": { "output": "dist" },
     "files": ["build/**/*", "node_modules/**/*", "package.json"],
     "mac": { "category": "public.app-category.productivity", "target": ["dmg", "zip"] },
     "win": { "target": ["nsis", "portable"] },
     "linux": { "target": ["AppImage", "deb"] }
   }

2. Setup code signing:
   - macOS: Obtain Apple Developer certificate, configure CSC_LINK and CSC_KEY_PASSWORD env vars
   - Windows: Obtain code signing certificate, configure CSC_LINK and CSC_KEY_PASSWORD env vars

3. Build for all platforms:
   - macOS: npm run dist -- --mac
   - Windows: npm run dist -- --win
   - Linux: npm run dist -- --linux

4. Setup auto-update with electron-updater:
   - Configure update server URL in main process
   - Add update check on app startup
   - Implement update download and install flow
   - Test update with mock server

5. Create distribution packages:
   - macOS: DMG installer with drag-and-drop
   - Windows: NSIS installer with auto-update support
   - Linux: AppImage and Deb package

6. Setup GitHub Releases for distribution:
   - Create GitHub release with version tag
   - Upload installers to release assets
   - Configure electron-updater to check GitHub releases

7. Create download page:
   - Add download links to web console
   - Detect user OS and suggest appropriate installer
   - Provide checksums for verification

8. Test installers:
   - Install on clean Windows 10/11 VM
   - Install on macOS 12+ (Intel and Apple Silicon)
   - Install on Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
   - Verify app launches and functions correctly
   - Test auto-update flow

Add build scripts to package.json: 'dist:mac', 'dist:win', 'dist:linux', 'dist:all'. Include publish configuration for GitHub Releases with owner, repo, and provider settings.

Ensure proper error handling and logging for update failures.

Include example .env.template file and GitHub Actions workflow snippets.

subtasks.description: Execute builds for all three platforms (Windows NSIS/portable, macOS DMG/zip, Linux AppImage/deb) with code signing, and configure GitHub Releases automation for distributing installers with auto-update support.
subtasks.details: Implement build and distribution pipeline: (1) Create platform-specific build scripts in package.json; (2) For macOS: Run 'electron-builder --mac' to generate DMG and zip with code signing and notarization (requires CSC_LINK, CSC_KEY_PASSWORD, APPLE_ID, APPLE_ID_PASSWORD); (3) For Windows: Run 'electron-builder --win' to generate NSIS installer and portable exe with code signing; (4) For Linux: Run 'electron-builder --linux' to generate AppImage and deb packages; (5) Configure GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) to build on push to tags (v*.*.*), using matrix strategy for multi-platform builds; (6) Use actions/upload-release-asset to upload all installers to GitHub Release; (7) Generate SHA256 checksums for all installers and include in release notes; (8) Configure electron-updater to use GitHub provider with owner and repo settings; (9) Test manual builds locally before CI/CD setup.

Integrate with GitHub Releases API to dynamically fetch latest version and download URLs.

Create test report with screenshots and logs.

tasks.description: Deploy Prometheus, Grafana, and configure dashboards for monitoring AlertHub services
tasks.details:
1. Deploy Prometheus using kube-prometheus-stack:
   helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
   helm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack -n monitoring --create-namespace

2. Configure ServiceMonitors for AlertHub services:
   - Create ServiceMonitor for notification-router (scrape /metrics on port 8080)
   - Create ServiceMonitor for integration-service (scrape /metrics on port 3000)
   - Create ServiceMonitor for admin-api (scrape /metrics on port 8080)

3. Create Grafana dashboards:
   - Dashboard 1: Service Health Overview
     * Panels: Service uptime, request rate, error rate, latency (p50, p95, p99)
     * Filters: Service, time range
   - Dashboard 2: Notification Metrics
     * Panels: Notifications submitted, delivered, failed, delivery time, channel breakdown
   - Dashboard 3: Infrastructure Metrics
     * Panels: PostgreSQL connections, Redis memory, Kafka lag, RabbitMQ queue depth
   - Dashboard 4: Resource Usage
     * Panels: CPU usage, memory usage, network I/O per service

4. Configure Prometheus alerts:
   - HighErrorRate: Alert when error rate > 5% for 5 minutes
   - HighLatency: Alert when p95 latency > 500ms for 5 minutes
   - ServiceDown: Alert when service is down for 1 minute
   - HighQueueDepth: Alert when RabbitMQ queue depth > 10000
   - DatabaseConnectionPoolExhausted: Alert when PostgreSQL connections > 90%

5. Setup Alertmanager:
   - Configure Slack integration for alerts
   - Configure email integration for critical alerts
   - Set up alert routing (critical -> Slack + email, warning -> Slack)

6. Configure log aggregation:
   - Deploy Loki for log aggregation
   - Configure Promtail to scrape logs from all pods
   - Add Loki data source to Grafana
   - Create log dashboard with filters (service, level, trace ID)

7. Setup distributed tracing (optional):
   - Deploy Jaeger or Tempo
   - Configure OpenTelemetry in services
   - Add tracing data source to Grafana
   - Create trace dashboard

subtasks.details: Execute: helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts && helm repo update && helm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack -n monitoring --create-namespace --set grafana.adminPassword=$GRAFANA_ADMIN_PW --set prometheus.prometheusSpec.serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues=false. Verify all pods are running in monitoring namespace. Export Grafana service and access UI to confirm deployment. Document the admin credentials and service endpoints.

subtasks.description: Deploy ServiceMonitor CRDs for notification-router, integration-service, and admin-api to enable Prometheus scraping of application metrics
subtasks.details: Create three ServiceMonitor YAML manifests: (1) notification-router-servicemonitor.yaml targeting port 8080 /metrics endpoint with labels matching notification-router service, (2) integration-service-servicemonitor.yaml targeting port 3000 /metrics endpoint, (3) admin-api-servicemonitor.yaml targeting port 8080 /metrics endpoint. Apply all ServiceMonitors to their respective namespaces. Configure scrape intervals of 30s and timeout of 10s. Include namespace selectors and matchLabels to correctly identify target services.

subtasks.description: Build four comprehensive Grafana dashboards: Service Health Overview, Notification Metrics, Infrastructure Metrics, and Resource Usage with PromQL queries and visualization panels
subtasks.details: Dashboard 1 (Service Health): Panels for up metric (uptime), rate(http_requests_total[5m]) (request rate), rate(http_requests_total{status=~'5..'}[5m]) (error rate), histogram_quantile(0.50|0.95|0.99, rate(http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) (latency percentiles). Dashboard 2 (Notifications): rate(notifications_submitted_total[5m]), rate(notifications_delivered_total[5m]), rate(notifications_failed_total[5m]), histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(notification_delivery_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])), notifications by channel breakdown. Dashboard 3 (Infrastructure): pg_stat_database_numbackends (PostgreSQL connections), redis_memory_used_bytes, kafka_consumergroup_lag, rabbitmq_queue_messages. Dashboard 4 (Resources): container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, container_memory_working_set_bytes, container_network_receive_bytes_total, container_network_transmit_bytes_total per service. Export dashboards as JSON and apply via ConfigMaps or Grafana provisioning.
subtasks.testStrategy: Access each dashboard in Grafana UI, verify all panels display data without errors, test time range filters, test service filter variables, confirm data refreshes correctly
subtasks.title: Create Grafana dashboards for service and infrastructure monitoring

subtasks.description: Create PrometheusRule CRDs with alert definitions for error rates, latency, service availability, queue depth, and database connections, then configure Alertmanager with Slack and email notification channels
subtasks.details: Create alerting-rules.yaml PrometheusRule with: HighErrorRate (expr: rate(http_requests_total{status=~'5..'}[5m]) / rate(http_requests_total[5m]) > 0.05 for 5m), HighLatency (expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) > 0.5 for 5m), ServiceDown (expr: up == 0 for 1m), HighQueueDepth (expr: rabbitmq_queue_messages > 10000 for 5m), DatabaseConnectionPoolExhausted (expr: pg_stat_database_numbackends / pg_settings_max_connections > 0.9 for 5m). Configure Alertmanager via alertmanager-config.yaml with Slack webhook receiver (critical and warning severity), email receiver (critical only), routing tree based on severity label. Update kube-prometheus-stack values to include custom alertmanager config.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify PrometheusRule is loaded in Prometheus UI under Alerts tab, trigger test alerts by simulating high error rate or service downtime, confirm alerts fire in Prometheus, verify alert notifications arrive in Slack and email channels

subtasks.description: Install Loki for centralized log storage and Promtail as a DaemonSet to collect logs from all AlertHub pods, then integrate with Grafana
subtasks.details: Deploy Loki using Helm: helm install loki grafana/loki-stack -n monitoring --set loki.persistence.enabled=true --set loki.persistence.size=10Gi --set promtail.enabled=true. Configure Promtail to scrape logs from all pods with labels matching AlertHub services. Add pipeline stages to parse JSON logs and extract trace_id, service, level fields. Add Loki as data source in Grafana (URL: http://loki:3100). Create log exploration dashboard with LogQL queries: {namespace='alerthub'} |= 'error', with filters for service name, log level, and trace_id. Configure log retention policy of 30 days.

tasks.details:
1. Setup E2E test framework:
